name: Bank Alert SMS Phishing
description: Bank fraud alert email that mirrors SMS content for smishing training.
subject: [Email copy of SMS alert] Suspicious activity on your account
text:
Chase Alert: Suspicious activity detected on your debit card ending in 4729. If this was not you, verify immediately: {{.URL}}

Reply STOP to unsubscribe from SMS alerts.

---
This is an email copy of the SMS alert sent to your registered phone number. If you did not opt in to SMS alerts, please verify your contact settings at the link above.

Chase Fraud Prevention
html:
CHASE
Chase Alerts • Now
Chase Alert: Suspicious activity detected on your debit card ending in 4729. If this was not you, verify immediately: chase.vfy-card.co

Reply STOP to unsubscribe.

This is an email copy of the SMS alert sent to your registered phone number. If you did not opt in to SMS alerts, please verify your contact settings at the link above.

Verify Activity

name: USPS Delivery SMS Phishing

text:
USPS: Your package is on hold due to an incomplete address. Please update your delivery information within 24 hours to avoid return:

{{.URL}}

name: SMS MFA Code Request
description: Social engineering SMS asking user to share their MFA code with IT helpdesk.
subject: [IT Helpdesk SMS] Security verification in progress
text:
IT Helpdesk: We are investigating suspicious activity on your corporate account. To verify your identity, please confirm the 6-digit code just sent to your phone via this form:

{{.URL}}

If you do not respond within 10 minutes, your account will be locked.

Mark Thompson
{{.OrgName}} IT Helpdesk
html:
{{.OrgName}} IT Helpdesk
{{.OrgName}} IT • Just now
Hi {{.FirstName}}, this is Mark from IT Helpdesk. We're investigating suspicious activity on your corporate account. To verify your identity, please confirm the 6-digit code we just sent you via this form:

it.{{.OrgName}}-verify.co/mfa

Respond within 10 minutes or your account will be locked.

This message is from the {{.OrgName}} IT Helpdesk. Do not share your MFA code with anyone outside of IT.

Verify Now

Mark Thompson • {{.OrgName}} IT Helpdesk
{{.Tracker}}

name: Amazon Order Cancellation SMS
description: Fake Amazon order cancellation SMS to panic users into clicking.
subject: [SMS] Your Amazon order has been cancelled
text:
Amazon: Your recent order of $849.99 has been CANCELLED due to payment issues. If you did not request this cancellation, reinstate your order here:

{{.URL}}

This link expires in 1 hour.

---

name: CEO Mobile Text Message
description: CEO text from a new number - smishing variant of CEO fraud.
subject: [Text message] New phone - Need quick favor
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}}, this is [CEO Name] - I got a new phone. Save this number. Are you in the office? I need you to handle something quickly and quietly.

I'm in a board meeting and can't call. Please respond via this secure channel:
{{.URL}}

I'll explain once you confirm. This is time sensitive.

Thanks
html:
Text Message
Unknown +1 (415) 555-0199
Hi {{.FirstName}}, this is [CEO Name] - I got a new phone. Save this number.
Are you in the office? I need you to handle something quickly and quietly.
I'm in a board meeting and can't call. Please respond via this secure channel: exec.secure-reply.co
I'll explain once you confirm. This is time sensitive. Thanks.

This message was forwarded to your email from an unknown number. Always verify via known channels before acting.

{{.Tracker}}
